claim: | Astro | JS/TS | No (islands opt-in) | Zero-JS HTML with selective hydration; ~5% of prerendered mobile pages

| Hugo | Go | No | Fastest build times — sub-second for thousands of pages
| Hugo | Go | No | Fastest build times — sub-second for thousands of pages ; ~18% of prerendered mobile pages

thousands of pages ; ~18% of prerendered mobile pages ; 78.4k GitHub stars | Large content sites, docs portals (Kubernetes docs runs on it)
| Next.js | JS/TS (React) | Yes | Hybrid SSG + SSR + ISR + edge in one stack
| Next.js | JS/TS (React) | Yes | Hybrid SSG + SSR + ISR + edge in one stack ; 130k stars, 17.9% framework market share
| Next.js | JS/TS (React) | Yes | Hybrid SSG + SSR + ISR + edge in one stack ; 130k stars, 17.9% framework market share | Sites that mix static marketing pages with app-like dynamic sections
Islands architecture means the JS payload matches what you actually need, not what the framework ships
Build times drop from minutes to milliseconds
ISR lets you regenerate content without a full rebuild
- Blog + free hosting, nothing fancy → Jekyll still powers GitHub Pages and works the same as a decade ago
- Pure documentation site → MkDocs (Python, docs-as-code) is narrower than Hugo and nicer for dev-written prose
- Hugo's templating (Go templates) is the hardest of the three to learn; expect developer pushback from JS-native teams
- Next.js ships JavaScript by default; reaching Astro-level Core Web Vitals takes deliberate tuning
